badges.name: All-Rounder
badges.description: Complete at least one lesson in every course

what-is-money.question: Why did people start using money instead of just trading items directly?
options: Because barter made it hard to find someone who wanted exactly what you had
Because money is shinier than goats

earning-vs-spending.question: What's one question you could ask yourself before spending money?

where-money-goes.question: Why might it be safer to keep money in a bank instead of under your mattress?

comparing-prices.question: If a 10-pack of pencils costs $4 and a 5-pack costs $2.50, which is the better deal per pencil?

why-save.question: What's one reason saving money could help you in the future?

savings-goal.question: If you wanted to save $60 in 6 months, how much would you need to save each month?

piggybank-vs-account.question: What's an advantage a savings account has over a piggy bank?
options: It can grow your money with interest
It's louder when you shake it

what-is-interest.question: If you save $200 at a 5% yearly interest rate, roughly how much extra money would you earn after one year?

compound-interest.question: Why is compound interest often compared to a snowball rolling downhill?

saving-for-something-big.question: At $10 saved per week, how many weeks would it take to save $120?

what-is-a-budget.question: Why might having a budget help you avoid running out of money unexpectedly?

three-buckets.question: If you got $20 and used a 50/40/10 split (spend/save/share), how much would go to saving?

tracking-your-money.question: What's one simple way you could track your spending this week?

trade-offs.question: Describe a trade-off you've had to make with your own money or time.

planning-a-purchase.question: What's something you'd like to save up for, and roughly how long do you think it would take?

what-is-investing.question: What's the main difference between investing and simply saving money?
options: Investing has more growth potential but more risk
Saving and investing are exactly the same

saving-vs-investing.question: Why might someone choose to save money instead of invest it, even though investing could grow it faster?

what-is-stock-market.question: In simple terms, what is a stock?
options: A small share of ownership in a company

risk-and-time.question: Why might starting to save at age 10 be more powerful than starting at age 30, even with smaller amounts?

money-toolkit-recap.question: Out of everything you've learned across these courses, what's one idea you want to remember most?
